items.title: China Has a 6th-Generation Fighter Jet. What Does It Mean for Boeing, Lockheed Martin, and Northrop Grumman?
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: China has tested two new sixth-generation fighter jets, while the U.S. is still flying fifth-generation jets. This could lead to an arms race, with the Pentagon needing to fund the development of new sixth-generation fighters by Boeing and Lockheed Martin to keep up with China's advancements.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Lockheed Martin is the likely favorite to build any new U.S. sixth-generation stealth fighter jets based on its experience with the F-22 and F-35.
Boeing is still in the running for the U.S. sixth-generation fighter jet contract, but its defense division is currently unprofitable, and it may or may not decide to sell it.
Boeing is still in the running for the U.S. sixth-generation fighter jet contract, but its defense division is currently unprofitable, and it may or may not decide to sell it.
Northrop Grumman has dropped out of the competition to develop the U.S. sixth-generation fighter jet, so it is no longer relevant to this story.
